page: Microsoft Azure E128-32as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor featuring 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with multithreading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external storage volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 3789.97 and high single-threaded compression speeds, including 41.52 GB/sec in gzip. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 59904.22 and a PassMark CPU score of 57357.48. Memory performance is robust, showing a PassMark memory score of 2952.22 and uncached read bandwidth of 102.69 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions, which contribute to strong performance in small-scale LLM inference, achieving 403.93 tokens/sec for text generation with a 135M parameter model. However, performance drops significantly on larger models, with text generation for a 70B model falling into the bottom tier at 2.49 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E128-32as_v7 offers a high density of memory relative to compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads that require massive memory footprints without needing a proportional scaling of vCPUs. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces baseline costs for memory-heavy applications that rely on network-attached storage. This profile is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: The Microsoft Azure E128-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores and a substantial 1024.0 GB of RAM, yielding 32.0 GB of memory per vCPU. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage for external disk configurations. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression capabilities, alongside moderate multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The processor supports AVX-512 and VNNI, enabling efficient execution of smaller language models, though performance degrades on larger models. This server is highly cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute, making it an excellent fit for large in-memory databases, enterprise caching systems, and heavy data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128-32as_v7 is an AMD-powered virtual machine with 32 vCPUs and 1024 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-heavy database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated AMD EPYC performance for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing 32.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or onboard GPUs
Well-suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise caching, and data processing
